content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of NNN integers. Some of the elements in the array are missing and are represented by the number −1-1−1. You need to replace each missing element (i.e. each −1-1−1) with an integer from the range [1,K][1, K][1,K] (both inclusive).

For each possible valid array (after filling all the missing positions), let f(A)f(A)f(A) denote the number of non-empty strictly increasing subsequences of the array.

Your task is to compute the sum of f(A)f(A)f(A) over all possible valid arrays. Since the answer can be very large, output it modulo 998244353998244353998244353.

**Note:**


A subsequence is obtained by deleting some (or none) of the elements without changing the order of the remaining elements.
A subsequence is strictly increasing if each element is strictly greater than the previous one.

Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK.
The next line contains NNN space separated integers A1,A2,...,ANA_1,A_2,...,A_NA1​,A2​,...,AN​.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the sum of the number of non-empty strictly increasing subsequences over all possible valid arrays, taken modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 500050005000.
